comments.body:
Case 1: Option to view Open Orders page by default:

Kindly get the following information from the merchant.

- How many times per day does the merchant switch from **All Orders** to **Open Orders**?
- How much time does the merchant estimate this change would save on a daily basis?
- Is the concern primarily the extra click required, or the time taken for the **All Orders** page to load before switching to **Open Orders**?
- Could you also understand the merchant's fulfillment workflow and explain how landing directly on the **Open Orders** view would improve their productivity?

comments.body:
Hello Ashur,

Good day!

Regarding your requirement about "Option to view the **Open Orders** page by default instead of the **All Orders** page", our Product Team is checking about this and to check for any possibilites, we need details for the following:

- How many times per day do you switch from **All Orders** to **Open Orders**?
- How much time do you estimate this change would save on a daily basis?
- Is the concern primarily the extra click required, or the time taken for the **All Orders** page to load before switching to **Open Orders**?
- Could you also share more details on the fulfillment workflow and let us know how landing directly on the **Open Orders** view would improve your productivity?


We have enabled the Auto Order update feature for your store. This will be applicable for the upcoming orders.

Best regards,
Sagar K S
PluginHive

PluginHive

comments.body:
Hi Sagar,

Thanks for getting back to us.

For our workflow, I personally switch from **All Orders** to **Open Orders** every time I open the app. We fulfil each order manually, as some orders are packed in flat-rate packaging while others require custom boxes (with specific dimensions and weight per box)

We usually process around **2–3 fulfilment batches per day**, and within each batch we are constantly checking which orders are ready to prepare. Because **All Orders** shows a combination of completed and non-completed/open orders, it makes the process more confusing and adds an unnecessary extra step each time.

Being able to set **Open Orders** as the default view would likely save around **1–2 minutes per fulfilment batch**. More importantly, it would reduce the chance of errors and lower the cognitive load when preparing orders.

The main concern is the combination of the extra click and the time taken for the All Orders page to load before switching to Open Orders. Landing directly on Open Orders would make the workflow cleaner, faster, and less prone to mistakes.

Kind regards,

 ![A&S Welding Logo](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0619/0402/7823/files/A_S_Logo_Original_-_Cropped_copy.png?v=1753840264)

**David Barkho**

Online Operations

A&S Welding & Electrical Repairs and Supplies

Unit 29, 72–80 Percival Road, Smithfield NSW 2164

aandswelding.com.au

(02) 9729 1127 / office@aandswelding.com.au

Thank you for your time on the call and for hearing our feedback.

As discussed, we appreciate your assistance regarding:
- Returning easily to filtered results on the PluginHive product view page (potential idea)
- Bulk/specific editing of dimension on Products page
- The correct process for re-generating cancelled labels
- Address auto-update functionality (now enabled)
- Default Open-View for orders (W.I.P)

comments.body:
**Call Summary:**
On the call, we understood the customer’s current fulfillment workflow and the steps they follow:

**Current Workflow:**
Open app → Switch to Open Orders → Open Order → Edit Package → Generate Label → Print Label → Mark as Fulfilled

During the call, I shared my screen and discussed **SLGP** with the customer. The customer was very happy with the feature, mentioned that it would save a lot of time, and showed interest in activating it. L3 team assistance is required for activation.

The customer also reported that order updates were not reflecting. Upon checking, the customer had not updated any orders after enabling auto order updates. The customer mentioned they would monitor it and get back to us if the concern persists.

**Customer App > Product page Feedback:**

1. When the customer searches for a product and edits it from the Product Details page, navigating back takes them to the default **All Orders** page instead of retaining the previous search/filter results. The customer requested that the search or filter state be saved so they can continue from the same filtered view rather than searching again.
2. The customer requested an option to select multiple items, including multiple variants, and update dimensions for each selected item. The customer is aware of the existing bulk edit option for dimensions when all products have the same dimensions, but they need the ability to assign different dimensions to each selected item in one place.


**Second Call Summary:**
The customer was on the **$9 Basic Plan**. The customer was informed that **SLGP** is available only for **$99 Enterprise Plan** customers.

The customer mentioned that the **$49** and **$99** plans are too expensive considering their sales volume and stated that the maximum they could consider is the **$29 Popular Plan**.

During the call, the customer selected the **$29 Popular Plan** and approved the charges.

I have attached the call recording for reference.

I have attached the call recording for reference.

comments.body:
Would like to get more details regarding **Case-1**: _“SLGP was provided as a solution to the Open Orders case.”_
Does this mean we are **not considering making changes to show the Open Orders page by default**?

Even though SLGP is enabled, the customer still has to navigate to the **Open** section to view the list of open orders and then click on the order number to generate labels.

**Customer’s current daily workflow:**
Open app → Click on **Open Orders** → Click on order number → Fulfil order → Again click on **Open** or switch orders using navigators → Fulfil orders

The customer is specifically looking to eliminate the **“clicking on Open Orders”** step and wants the **Open Orders page to be displayed by default** when they access the app.
comments.html_body:
Would like to get more details regarding Case-1: “SLGP was provided as a solution to the Open Orders case.”
Does this mean we are not considering making changes to show the Open Orders page by default?
 
Even though SLGP is enabled, the customer still has to navigate to the Open section to view the list of open orders and then click on the order number to generate labels.
 
Customer’s current daily workflow:
Open app → Click on Open Orders → Click on order number → Fulfil order → Again click on Open or switch orders using navigators → Fulfil orders
 
The customer is specifically looking to eliminate the “clicking on Open Orders” step and wants the Open Orders page to be displayed by default when they access the app.

comments.body:
Hi Manohar,

Thank you for the update.

We have tested the new **Generate Label and Fulfil** function, and it is working.

However, we are having one issue. After pressing **Generate Label and Fulfil**, the label is generated and the order is fulfilled successfully, but the page does not update automatically.

The button in the top left remains as **Generate Label** instead of changing to **Print Documents**. We have to manually refresh the page for the change to appear, even though the order has already been fulfilled in the background.

Could you please check if the page can automatically refresh or update after the label generation and fulfilment process is completed? This would make the one-click workflow much smoother.

Kind regards,

 ![A&S Welding Logo](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0619/0402/7823/files/A_S_Logo_Original_-_Cropped_copy.png?v=1753840264)

**David Barkho**

Online Operations

comments.html_body:
Hi Manohar,

Thank you for the update.

We have tested the new Generate Label and Fulfil function, and it is working.

However, we are having one issue. After pressing Generate Label and Fulfil, the label is generated and the order is fulfilled successfully, but the page does not update automatically.

The button in the top left remains as Generate Label instead of changing to Print Documents. We have to manually refresh the page for the change to appear, even though the order has already been fulfilled in the background.

Could you please check if the page can automatically refresh or update after the label generation and fulfilment process is completed? This would make the one-click workflow much smoother.

Kind regards,

David Barkho

Online Operations

A&S Welding & Electrical Repairs and Supplies

Unit 29, 72–80 Percival Road, Smithfield NSW 2164

aandswelding.com.au

(02) 9729 1127 / office@aandswelding.com.au

🤖 Auto-generated draft — customer reply (probation mode)
Product: shopify-mcsl | Signal: form_field | Confidence: MEDIUM | Context: 26 message(s)
─────────────────────────────────────────────

Hello David,

Thank you for testing the SLGP feature and sharing this detailed feedback. It's great to hear that the label generation and fulfilment are completing successfully.

Regarding the page not updating automatically — you are right that after 'Generate Label and Fulfil' completes, the button should transition to 'Print Documents' without needing a manual refresh. This is a UI behaviour gap in the current SLGP flow, and we have flagged it with our product team for review. We will update you once we have more information on whether this can be addressed.

For now, a manual page refresh will load the updated state and give you access to the Print Documents button straight away.

We are also continuing to follow up with the team on the other open items — the Open Orders default view and the Product page enhancements — and will update you as soon as we hear back.

Thanks & Regards,
Team PluginHive

⚡ This response was generated automatically by PluginHive AI Support. A team member will follow up if you need further assistance.

comments.body:
Hello David,

I’m following up on Sagar’s previous email regarding the Generate Label and Fulfil workflow.

The expected behavior is that when Generate Label and Fulfil is clicked, the app should generate the label, update the order status to Fulfilled, and automatically change the option to Print Documents without requiring a complete browser page refresh.

If this is not working as expected, could you please share a short video recording of fulfilling one order and explain what exactly is happening on your end? We’ll review it with the team and check this further.

If it is easier, I’m happy to schedule a call and review this together. Please use the appointment link [CLICK HERE] to book a slot based on your availability.

Looking forward to hearing back from you.

Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Hi Manohar,

Sorry for the late reply, I have been busy.

I have booked a call with you using the appointment link provided:

**Booked From:** June 17, 2026, 1:15 pm
**Booked To:** June 17, 2026, 1:45 pm

I currently have an order ready for fulfilment, which I have not generated a label for yet, so I can show you the issue during the call.

We have also noticed another issue. When we split fulfilment in Shopify Admin, where part of the order is marked as **On Hold** and the remaining part is **Unfulfilled**, the order in PluginHive does update to reflect the correct unfulfilled items.

However, when we fulfil the order in PluginHive, the same issue occurs where we have to refresh the page manually. After refreshing, PluginHive shows that the order has been fulfilled, but the tracking information does not copy over to Shopify.

Kind regards,

 ![A&S Welding Logo](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0619/0402/7823/files/A_S_Logo_Original_-_Cropped_copy.png?v=1753840264)

comments.body:
Back to L3 - Case and Requirement to be checked with Dev_Team

- Customer mentioned that the SLGP is working fine until the label generation and fulfilment. Once it is completed, the process is not getting refreshed automatically in the order summary page and the customer has to refresh the page > it will go back to All Orders page > customer has to click on the order number again > then they will see Print Documents option
The customer is complaining this is an additional step and does not support the whole purpose of SLGP
Please check the current working in the customer store in the video recording attached

- Customer is partially fulfilling the orders (some products now and some later when the stock is available) and they are following the steps of ptting the products to OnHold and those updates are considered in the app. When the customer uses SLGP, the label generation is happening correctly for the current available products however the fulfillment is failing with the error message "Invalid fulfillment order line item quantity requested."
Currently, the partial fulfilment feature is not enabled for the store and i suspect the fulfilment failure is due to this reason.
So, please enable this feature to the store


Please check both and provide an update. Attached the video recording for reference.
comments.html_body:
Back to L3 - Case and Requirement to be checked with Dev_Team
 
  • Customer mentioned that the SLGP is working fine until the label generation and fulfilment. Once it is completed, the process is not getting refreshed automatically in the order summary page and the customer has to refresh the page > it will go back to All Orders page > customer has to click on the order number again > then they will see Print Documents option
    The customer is complaining this is an additional step and does not support the whole purpose of SLGP
    Please check the current working in the customer store in the video recording attached
     
  • Customer is partially fulfilling the orders (some products now and some later when the stock is available) and they are following the steps of ptting the products to OnHold and those updates are considered in the app. When the customer uses SLGP, the label generation is happening correctly for the current available products however the fulfillment is failing with the error message "Invalid fulfillment order line item quantity requested."
    Currently, the partial fulfilment feature is not enabled for the store and i suspect the fulfilment failure is due to this reason. 
    So, please enable this feature to the store
 
Please check both and provide an update. Attached the video recording for reference.

comments.body:
Hello David,

comments.body:
Moving this case back to L3.

**Case:** SLIP page not refreshing after clicking "Generate and Fulfill Label"

**Details:**
The customer reports that after clicking the **Generate and Fulfill Label** button on the SLIP page, the label generation process is initiated successfully, and pop-up messages indicate that label generation and manifest generation have been completed.

However, even after waiting for more than a minute, the page does not refresh automatically to reflect the updated order status or display the print document options. The customer has to either navigate back to the All Orders page or manually refresh the browser for the changes to appear.

The issue is occurring in both Chrome and Safari browsers. A video demonstrating the behavior has been attached.

**Expected Behavior:**
Once the label is generated and the order is fulfilled, the SLIP page should automatically update to reflect the fulfilled status and allow the customer to access and print documents without requiring a manual page refresh.

Could you please review the attached video and share your findings?

**Store Details:**
Store URL: aandswelding.myshopify.com
Account ID: a1b5f59b-4478-474a-888b-b1dd1bffaa97

comments.body:
Dev Needed case moving to L3 to get an ETA

Case: Customer shared feedback related to the app>products page
1. When the customer searches for a product and edits it from the Product Details page, navigating back takes them to the default **All Orders** page instead of retaining the previous search/filter results. The customer requested that the search or filter state be saved so they can continue from the same filtered view rather than searching again.
2. The customer requested an option to select multiple items, including multiple variants, and update dimensions for each selected item. The customer is aware of the existing bulk edit option for dimensions when all products have the same dimensions, but they need the ability to assign different dimensions to each selected item in one place.
comments.html_body:
Dev Needed case moving to L3 to get an ETA
 
Case: Customer shared feedback related to the app>products page
  1. When the customer searches for a product and edits it from the Product Details page, navigating back takes them to the default All Orders page instead of retaining the previous search/filter results. The customer requested that the search or filter state be saved so they can continue from the same filtered view rather than searching again.
  2. The customer requested an option to select multiple items, including multiple variants, and update dimensions for each selected item. The customer is aware of the existing bulk edit option for dimensions when all products have the same dimensions, but they need the ability to assign different dimensions to each selected item in one place.
